# Auth, Roles & Row-Level Security

I have everything I need. The custom-access-token-hook detail I'll rely on: the hook receives `event->'claims'`, mutates, returns the event; required claims are preserved by the platform when you only `jsonb_set` your additions (the simple pattern above works). I have enough verified detail to write the section now.

A note on the single-account-dual-role requirement: the canonical Supabase RBAC example uses a single scalar `user_role` claim, but this platform needs guest + host simultaneously, so I'll design an **array** claim (`app_roles`) plus a separate `active_host_id` claim, which is the correct adaptation.

## Auth, Roles & Row-Level Security (Supabase)

This subsystem governs identity, role representation, and data-access enforcement for {{PLATFORM_NAME}}. The guiding principle: **RLS is the security boundary, not the app.** The Expo app and Next.js dashboards talk to Postgres through the same anon/auth keys; every table is `enable row level security` with `force row level security`, and the service-role key is used *only* inside Edge Functions / SECURITY DEFINER RPCs, never shipped to a client.

### 1. Role representation

`auth.users` stays untouched (managed by GoTrue). Identity and roles live in `public`:

```sql
-- One row per human, 1:1 with auth.users
create table public.profiles (
  id              uuid primary key references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
  display_name    text not null,
  avatar_path     text,                 -- storage object path, not URL
  preferred_locale text not null default 'ar' check (preferred_locale in ('ar','fr','en')),
  phone_e164      text unique,          -- +213...
  phone_verified_at timestamptz,
  created_at      timestamptz not null default now()
);

create type public.app_role as enum
  ('guest','host_individual','host_hotel','hotel_staff','admin','super_admin');

-- A single account can hold MANY roles (guest + host simultaneously)
create table public.user_roles (
  user_id   uuid not null references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
  role      public.app_role not null,
  granted_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  primary key (user_id, role)
);

-- Host identity (KYC + payout). One per host account.
create type public.host_kind as enum ('individual','hotel');
create type public.verification_status as enum ('unverified','pending','verified','rejected');

create table public.host_profiles (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  owner_id          uuid not null unique references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
  kind              public.host_kind not null,
  legal_name        text,
  id_doc_path       text,                          -- CNI / passport in private bucket
  identity_status   public.verification_status not null default 'unverified',
  payout_status     public.verification_status not null default 'unverified',
  payout_rib        text,                          -- 20-digit Algerian RIB (CCP/bank)
  payout_method     text check (payout_method in ('ccp','bank','baridimob')),
  created_at        timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- Hotel staff -> which hotel, with sub-capability
create type public.staff_capability as enum ('reception','manager');
create table public.hotel_staff (
  user_id     uuid not null references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
  property_id uuid not null references public.properties(id) on delete cascade,
  capability  public.staff_capability not null,
  primary key (user_id, property_id)
);
```

**JWT custom claims.** The single-scalar-role example in the Supabase RBAC docs is insufficient here because one account is both `guest` and a host. I use a **Custom Access Token Hook** that injects an *array* of roles plus the host id. (Verified against Supabase's custom-access-token-hook / RBAC guide — same `jsonb_set(event->'claims', …)` mechanism, adapted to an array.)

```sql
create or replace function public.custom_access_token_hook(event jsonb)
returns jsonb language plpgsql stable as $$
declare
  claims    jsonb;
  uid       uuid := (event->>'user_id')::uuid;
  roles     text[];
  hp_id     uuid;
begin
  select array_agg(role::text) into roles from public.user_roles where user_id = uid;
  select id into hp_id from public.host_profiles where owner_id = uid;

  claims := event->'claims';
  claims := jsonb_set(claims, '{app_roles}', to_jsonb(coalesce(roles, array['guest'])));
  claims := jsonb_set(claims, '{host_id}',  coalesce(to_jsonb(hp_id), 'null'::jsonb));
  return jsonb_set(event, '{claims}', claims);
end; $$;

-- Grants required by GoTrue (per Supabase docs)
grant usage on schema public to supabase_auth_admin;
grant execute on function public.custom_access_token_hook to supabase_auth_admin;
revoke execute on function public.custom_access_token_hook from authenticated, anon, public;
grant select on public.user_roles, public.host_profiles to supabase_auth_admin;
create policy "auth_admin reads roles" on public.user_roles
  as permissive for select to supabase_auth_admin using (true);
create policy "auth_admin reads host_profiles" on public.host_profiles
  as permissive for select to supabase_auth_admin using (true);
```

Enable under **Authentication → Hooks → Customize Access Token**. Claims land in the JWT as `app_roles: ["guest","host_individual"]` and `host_id`. **Note:** custom claims refresh only on token refresh, so after granting a role server-side, force a session refresh client-side (`supabase.auth.refreshSession()`).

Helper predicates used by every policy below (declared `security definer set search_path = ''`, `stable`):

```sql
create or replace function auth.has_role(r text) returns boolean
language sql stable as $$
  select coalesce(auth.jwt() -> 'app_roles' ? r, false);
$$;

create or replace function auth.is_staff() returns boolean
language sql stable as $$ select auth.has_role('admin') or auth.has_role('super_admin'); $$;

create or replace function auth.my_host_id() returns uuid
language sql stable as $$ select nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'host_id','null')::uuid; $$;
```

Single account = guest + host is now trivial: granting `host_individual` adds a `user_roles` row and creates a `host_profiles` row; the same JWT carries both `guest` and `host_individual`. The app renders "Guest mode / Host mode" purely as a UI toggle — no second login, no second account.

### 2. Email + phone (OTP) verification & gating

**Email:** standard GoTrue confirm-email on signup. Required before any role beyond `guest` is granted.

**Phone OTP:** GoTrue phone provider via SMS. Provider reality for Algeria (+213) verified against Supabase phone-login docs (supported: **Twilio**, **Twilio Verify**, **Vonage**, **MessageBird**, **TextLocal**):
- **Twilio Verify** — recommended primary; best +213 deliverability/compliance handling, and Verify owns the OTP lifecycle.
- **Vonage / MessageBird** — viable fallbacks; confirm an alphanumeric/long-code route to Algeria with the carrier before launch.
- Algerian A2P SMS is operator-gated (Mobilis/Djezzy/Ooredoo); deliverability is the real risk, so build a **WhatsApp OTP fallback** (Twilio WhatsApp) behind the same `verify_phone` Edge Function, and keep `phone_verified_at` as the single source of truth regardless of channel.

GoTrue's default 60-second resend throttle / 1-hour expiry are kept; an additional per-IP + per-user rate limit lives in the Edge Function wrapper to blunt SMS-pumping fraud.

**Gating (enforced in DB, not UI).** Two independent gates, both required for a host:

```sql
-- A listing cannot leave 'pending' for 'approved' unless host identity verified.
-- Enforced in the admin moderation RPC (sec. 5), which checks:
--   host_profiles.identity_status = 'verified'
-- A host cannot be paid out unless payout account verified.
alter table public.properties
  add constraint chk_publish_requires_listed
  check (status <> 'approved' or true);  -- structural; real check in RPC + trigger below
```

A `before update` trigger on `properties` rejects transition to `approved` when the owning `host_profiles.identity_status <> 'verified'`. A `before insert` trigger on `payouts` (sec. 5) rejects creation when `payout_status <> 'verified'`. This makes the gate impossible to bypass from any client.

### 3. RLS policy design per table

All tables: `alter table … enable row level security;`. Admins/super_admins get a blanket bypass via a `for all` policy using `auth.is_staff()`. Listed below per table (representative snippets; the staff bypass `… or auth.is_staff()` is appended to read/write policies but shown once for brevity).

#### `properties` (draft→pending→approved/rejected/suspended)
- **SELECT:** public/guest sees only `status='approved' and is_published`; host sees all own rows (any status); hotel_staff sees their hotel's row; admin all.
- **INSERT:** only a host role, and only with `owner_host_id = auth.my_host_id()`.
- **UPDATE:** owning host while not suspended; staff `manager` may edit hotel content; `status` column changes are blocked for clients (only the moderation RPC, running as definer, sets `approved/rejected/suspended`).
- **DELETE:** owning host only if `status='draft'`; otherwise soft-delete via admin.

```sql
create policy prop_public_read on public.properties
for select to anon, authenticated
using ( (status = 'approved' and is_published)
        or owner_host_id = auth.my_host_id()
        or exists (select 1 from public.hotel_staff s
                   where s.user_id = auth.uid() and s.property_id = id)
        or auth.is_staff() );

create policy prop_host_insert on public.properties
for insert to authenticated
with check ( owner_host_id = auth.my_host_id()
             and (auth.has_role('host_individual') or auth.has_role('host_hotel')) );

create policy prop_host_update on public.properties
for update to authenticated
using ( owner_host_id = auth.my_host_id() and status <> 'suspended' )
with check ( owner_host_id = auth.my_host_id() );
```

#### `room_types`, `availability`
Both scoped through parent `properties.owner_host_id`. Guests read only rows whose parent property is approved+published (needed to show price/calendar). Hosts and `manager` staff write; `reception` staff may update `availability` (block/unblock dates, walk-in) but **not** `room_types` pricing.

```sql
create policy avail_public_read on public.availability
for select to anon, authenticated
using ( exists (select 1 from public.properties p
                where p.id = property_id and p.status='approved' and p.is_published) );

create policy avail_write on public.availability
for all to authenticated
using ( exists (select 1 from public.properties p
                where p.id = property_id
                  and (p.owner_host_id = auth.my_host_id()
                       or exists (select 1 from public.hotel_staff s
                                  where s.user_id=auth.uid() and s.property_id=p.id))) )
with check ( exists (select 1 from public.properties p
                     where p.id = property_id
                       and (p.owner_host_id = auth.my_host_id()
                            or exists (select 1 from public.hotel_staff s
                                       where s.user_id=auth.uid() and s.property_id=p.id))) );
```

#### `bookings`
- **SELECT:** the guest who booked (`guest_id = auth.uid()`), OR the host of the booked property, OR staff of that hotel.
- **INSERT:** *blocked for clients.* Bookings are created only by the `create_booking` RPC (sec. 5) so inventory hold + price snapshot + payment init are atomic. (Client-side `insert` policy = `with check (false)`.)
- **UPDATE:** guest may only move own booking to `cancelled` within policy window; host/manager may `confirm`/`reject`/mark `no_show`; reception may `check_in`/`check_out`. Status machine enforced by trigger.

```sql
create policy bk_select on public.bookings
for select to authenticated
using ( guest_id = auth.uid()
        or exists (select 1 from public.properties p
                   where p.id = property_id
                     and ( p.owner_host_id = auth.my_host_id()
                           or exists (select 1 from public.hotel_staff s
                                      where s.user_id=auth.uid() and s.property_id=p.id))) );

create policy bk_no_client_insert on public.bookings
for insert to authenticated with check (false);   -- RPC-only
```

#### `reviews`
- **SELECT:** public read for reviews attached to approved properties.
- **INSERT:** only by a guest who has a `completed` booking for that property (`exists` subquery), one review per booking.
- **UPDATE/DELETE:** author only, within an edit window; host gets a one-time *reply* via a separate `review_replies` table, not by editing the review.

```sql
create policy rev_insert on public.reviews
for insert to authenticated
with check ( author_id = auth.uid()
  and exists (select 1 from public.bookings b
              where b.id = booking_id and b.guest_id = auth.uid()
                and b.status = 'completed') );
```

#### `favorites`
Pure per-user: `using (user_id = auth.uid())` for all of select/insert/delete; no update.

```sql
create policy fav_all on public.favorites
for all to authenticated
using (user_id = auth.uid()) with check (user_id = auth.uid());
```

#### `conversations` / `messages`
- Conversation has `guest_id` and `host_id`. **SELECT/INSERT** allowed if `auth.uid()` is a participant (or staff of the host's hotel).
- Messages: read if member of the parent conversation; insert only as `sender_id = auth.uid()` and member; no update/delete (immutability for dispute evidence) except a `deleted_at` soft flag by the sender.

```sql
create policy msg_select on public.messages
for select to authenticated
using ( exists (select 1 from public.conversations c
                where c.id = conversation_id
                  and (c.guest_id = auth.uid() or c.host_id = auth.uid()
                       or exists (select 1 from public.hotel_staff s
                                  join public.properties p on p.id=s.property_id
                                  where s.user_id=auth.uid() and c.host_id = p.owner_host_id))) );

create policy msg_insert on public.messages
for insert to authenticated
with check ( sender_id = auth.uid()
  and exists (select 1 from public.conversations c
              where c.id = conversation_id
                and (c.guest_id = auth.uid() or c.host_id = auth.uid())) );
```

#### `transactions`, `payouts`
Read-only to clients. Guest sees transactions tied to own bookings; host sees transactions/payouts for own properties. **No client INSERT/UPDATE** — written by payment webhook + payout-run Edge Functions (service role). Snippet: `for select using (host_id = auth.my_host_id())` on payouts; `with check(false)` on writes.

#### `reports` / `disputes`
- **INSERT:** any authenticated user about a booking/property/user they're connected to (`reporter_id = auth.uid()`).
- **SELECT:** reporter sees own; admin sees all. Hosts do **not** see who reported them.
- **UPDATE:** admin/super_admin only (status, resolution).

#### `notifications`
Per-user read (`user_id = auth.uid()`), update only `read_at`; inserts come from triggers/Edge Functions (definer), `with check(false)` for clients.

#### Master tables `wilayas` (58), `property_types`, `amenities`
Public read to `anon, authenticated`; writes restricted to `super_admin`.

```sql
create policy master_read on public.wilayas for select to anon, authenticated using (true);
create policy master_write on public.wilayas for all to authenticated
  using (auth.has_role('super_admin')) with check (auth.has_role('super_admin'));
```

**Reception vs manager** difference is realized through `hotel_staff.capability`: policies that mutate pricing/content (`room_types`, property edits, payout config) add `and s.capability='manager'`; operational policies (`availability` blocking, booking `check_in`/`check_out`) allow both. This is enforced inside the status-transition trigger functions, which read `hotel_staff.capability`, since the *which-transition-is-allowed* logic is richer than a single policy boolean.

**Admin / super_admin bypass** is a single `for all … using(auth.is_staff())` policy per table; `super_admin`-only actions (role grants, master-table writes, hard deletes) additionally check `auth.has_role('super_admin')`.

### 4. Storage bucket policies

Three buckets:

| bucket | public? | contents |
|---|---|---|
| `property-photos` | public-read | listing images |
| `avatars` | public-read | profile pictures |
| `kyc-docs` | **private** | ID documents, RIB proof |

**Folder-per-owner convention:** object path = `{owner_id}/{property_id}/{uuid}.webp` (photos) and `{user_id}/avatar.webp` (avatars). RLS on `storage.objects` keys off the first path segment via `storage.foldername(name)[1]`.

```sql
-- Upload: only into your own folder, only hosts, into property-photos
create policy "photo upload own folder" on storage.objects
for insert to authenticated
with check ( bucket_id = 'property-photos'
  and (storage.foldername(name))[1] = auth.uid()::text
  and (auth.has_role('host_individual') or auth.has_role('host_hotel') or auth.has_role('hotel_staff')) );

-- Public read of property photos & avatars
create policy "photo public read" on storage.objects
for select to anon, authenticated
using ( bucket_id in ('property-photos','avatars') );

-- KYC: private. Upload to own folder; READ only by admins (via signed URL from an RPC)
create policy "kyc upload own" on storage.objects
for insert to authenticated
with check ( bucket_id='kyc-docs' and (storage.foldername(name))[1] = auth.uid()::text );
create policy "kyc admin read" on storage.objects
for select to authenticated
using ( bucket_id='kyc-docs' and auth.is_staff() );
```

`kyc-docs` is never publicly readable; the moderation UI fetches a short-TTL **signed URL** (`createSignedUrl`, 60–120 s) generated inside the admin RPC after an `auth.is_staff()` check. Public photos are served directly; if you want hotlink control, switch `property-photos` to private + signed URLs too, but for v1 public-read is acceptable and cheaper on egress.

### 5. Logic that RLS cannot express → Edge Functions / SECURITY DEFINER RPCs

RLS is row-level and declarative; multi-row atomic invariants and external calls belong here.

- **`create_booking(property_id, room_type_id, check_in, check_out, pax)`** — SECURITY DEFINER RPC. In one transaction: re-validate availability, `select … for update` on the date rows to **hold inventory** (decrement/insert a `held` availability span with a TTL), snapshot the nightly price in DZD, insert the `bookings` row as `pending_payment`, return `booking_id`. Prevents the double-book race RLS can't see. Then an **Edge Function** calls Chargily Pay v2 `POST https://api.chargily.com/v2/checkouts` with `{ amount, currency:"dzd", payment_method:"edahabia"|"cib", success_url, failure_url, webhook_endpoint, locale, metadata:[{booking_id}] }` (auth via `Authorization: Bearer <secret>`), and returns `checkout_url` for the WebView. (Endpoint/fields verified against Chargily dev docs.) Baridi Pay/BaridiMob QR runs through the parallel branch of the same function.
- **`chargily-webhook` Edge Function** — verifies the `signature` header = HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body with the API secret (per Chargily webhook docs), is **idempotent** on event id, then flips `transactions` + `bookings` (`paid`/`failed`), releases or confirms the inventory hold, and inserts a `notifications` row. Service-role; not reachable by clients.
- **`expire_holds` (scheduled)** — cron Edge Function releasing `held` availability whose TTL passed and marking the booking `expired`.
- **Admin moderation RPC `moderate_property(property_id, action, reason)`** — definer; checks `auth.is_staff()`, enforces the identity-verified gate before `approve`, writes the new `status`, and writes an **audit row** (sec. 6) in the same transaction.
- **`grant_host_role(user_id)` / staff management** — `super_admin`-checked definer RPCs that mutate `user_roles`/`hotel_staff` (clients can't write these tables directly).
- **Payout run** — scheduled Edge Function that aggregates `completed` bookings per host, checks `payout_status='verified'`, inserts `payouts`, and integrates the disbursement (manual CCP/bank file for v1). The `payouts` `before insert` trigger enforces the verification gate at the DB level too.

### 6. Immutable audit logging

```sql
create table public.audit_log (
  id          bigint generated always as identity primary key,
  actor_id    uuid not null,            -- auth.uid() of admin
  action      text not null,            -- 'property.suspend','booking.refund','user.delete'
  target_type text not null,
  target_id   uuid,
  reason      text,
  metadata    jsonb,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now()
);
alter table public.audit_log enable row level security;
-- No INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policy for anon/authenticated => clients can't touch it at all.
create policy audit_admin_read on public.audit_log
  for select to authenticated using (auth.is_staff());
revoke update, delete on public.audit_log from authenticated, anon;
```

Rows are written **only** from SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (suspend/refund/delete/moderate), so the writer runs as table owner while clients have no write policy at all — append-only by construction. Immutability is hardened by an `before update or delete` trigger that `raise exception`s, and by keeping the table out of any client grant. (For tamper-evidence you can add a `prev_hash`/`row_hash` chain, but the no-policy + reject-trigger combo already makes it append-only from every client path.)

### 7. Realtime

Realtime is opt-in per table via the `supabase_realtime` publication; **RLS applies to Realtime** (Postgres Changes authorize each row against the subscriber's JWT), so a client only receives change events for rows its SELECT policy already permits.

- **`messages`** — chat; subscriber filtered to their `conversation_id`s by the `msg_select` policy.
- **`bookings`** — status changes pushed to the guest and the property's host/staff (their `bk_select` policy scopes it).
- **`notifications`** — per-user feed; `user_id = auth.uid()` policy ensures isolation.
- **`availability`** — optional, to live-update a property's calendar while a host edits.

```sql
alter publication supabase_realtime
  add table public.messages, public.bookings, public.notifications, public.availability;
```

Because broadcast authorization reuses the same policies, there's no separate channel ACL to maintain: tightening a table's RLS automatically tightens what streams over Realtime. For the chat UI we use Realtime **Broadcast from Database** (trigger → `realtime.broadcast_changes`) on `messages` so typing/read receipts ride the same authorized channel without widening table grants.

---
**Sources relied on:** [Supabase Custom Claims & RBAC](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/custom-claims-and-role-based-access-control-rbac), [Supabase Custom Access Token Hook](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/auth-hooks/custom-access-token-hook), [Supabase Phone Login](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/phone-login), [Chargily Pay v2 — Create Checkout](https://dev.chargily.com/pay-v2/api-reference/checkouts/create), [Chargily Pay v2 — Webhooks](https://dev.chargily.com/pay-v2/webhooks), [Expo Localization / RTL](https://docs.expo.dev/guides/localization/).
